resources.string: FlipToFocus requires Usage Access permission to detect when distracting social apps are launched in the foreground. This data is processed strictly on your device and is never transmitted or collected.

FlipToFocus requires the Draw Over Other Apps permission to place a visual focus screen on top of distracting applications, helping you stay committed to your break. Your screen content is private and never recorded.

FlipToFocus uses the Accessibility service only to read the NAME of the app currently in the foreground, so it can show your focus screen when you open an app you chose to block. It never reads, collects, or transmits the text or content on your screen. All processing stays on your device.

Detects when an app you chose to block is opened, so FlipToFocus can show your focus challenge. It only uses the foreground app\'s name and never reads or stores your screen content – everything stays on your device.
Lets FlipToFocus detect blocked apps on-device to enforce your focus breaks.

FlipToFocus gently interrupts distracting apps and asks you to complete a short offline break challenge before you continue. Place your phone face-down, stay still for the timer, and get back to what matters. Everything runs on your device.
Blocking is ready to go. You can adjust your blocklist and challenge settings at any time from the app.
When on, the phone must be flat and face-down. When off, flat face-up also counts.
Lower is stricter - smaller movements will reset the timer.
All data stays on your device. FlipToFocus has no internet access, no analytics, and no tracking.
%1$s is paused. Complete your break to continue.
Place your phone face-down and keep still
This will unlock the app and log the break as abandoned. You\'re always in control.
Watching for distracting apps on this device.
